claim: A lean CLAUDE.md file (100-200 lines max) covering project purpose, file structure, conventions, and dev commands prevents context bloat and token waste
actionable: Create a CLAUDE.md with project description, key paths, build/test commands, and coding conventions; keep it under 200 lines
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Senior Software Engineer Tips)

claim: Plan mode prevents bad architecture: reviewing Claude's implementation plan before execution catches misaligned solutions early, saving hours of debugging
actionable: Always use plan mode; review and edit the plan before Claude writes code to ensure correct approach before line changes
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Senior Software Engineer Tips)

claim: Using /clear between tasks prevents lost-in-the-middle degradation; context performance decays as history grows, so each task should start with a fresh context window
actionable: Run /clear after completing each task; don't treat a Claude Code session as a persistent conversation
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Senior Software Engineer Tips)

claim: A validation loop (build + test + type check running after every Claude edit) lets the agent self-correct without human intervention
actionable: Wire up build, test, and type-check hooks in your Claude.md config; let them run automatically after each edit
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Senior Software Engineer Tips)

claim: Git worktrees enable parallel Claude Code sessions without conflicts; each session gets its own clean working tree for independent feature/bug work
actionable: Use git worktrees to check out multiple branches simultaneously; run separate Claude Code sessions on each worktree
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Senior Software Engineer Tips)

claim: Sub-agents are Claude sessions that your main session delegates isolated work to; each has its own context and rules, keeping the main session clean
actionable: Define sub-agents in .claude.agents folder for specialized tasks (security review, DB queries); delegate from main session
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Senior Software Engineer Tips)

claim: MCP servers can bloat context; be cautious about adding multiple MCPs since their tool definitions consume significant context tokens
actionable: Use MCP servers sparingly; monitor context usage and prefer targeted, minimal MCP setups
source_title: How I use Claude Code (Senior Software Engineer Tips)
